milestone: Zero-Holder Scenes -- the real white-box source found and fixed (renderer/slideRenderer.js)
slice: A direct, confirmed follow-up found by reproducing the user's actual live World shape: a zero-Place Scene with a Scene-hosted dark Colour-fill background and a Free-hosted gold octagon Decoration Shape still showed a white box in Creator after the previous _resolveBorder fix. Root cause: render()'s own composition-selection code falls to an 'else' branch that unconditionally paints the classic pre-Artwork-Theme 'story picture panel' rectangle whenever the resolved border is null -- correct for a plain Story Theme, wrong for a zero-Holder Artwork Theme Scene which has no picture area at all. Fixed with a new branch that draws nothing when the active Layout's holders count is explicitly 0. Verified via a real git stash A/B test on the user's exact World shape confirming the panel-rect center pixel goes from pure white to the theme's own page colour, with zero regression across the full Creator/Builder suite and both goldenBuild.js suites (Museum Gallery's real Holder still renders its Frame/mat/Wax Seal unchanged).
